sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I have a clear picture:
- Wall starts at col 52 at top (rows 1-19), col 4 at middle (rows 24-39), col 16 at bottom (rows 44-63)
- Figure at cols 18-21 (rows 52-57)
- Gap at cols 28-29 (rows 40-43)
The figure is at col 18, the gap is at col 28. The figure needs to move right by 10 more columns to align with the gap.
Each click on the lower blue block moves the figure right by 4 columns. So I need about 2.5 more clicks, meaning 3 more clicks to get the figure to col 30 (past the gap).
But wait, the wall also changes shape. At row 52, the wall starts at col 20. The figure is at col 18, so it's just to the left of the wall boundary. After more clicks, the wall boundary at row 52 will move right, and the figure will move right with it.
Let me think about the goal differently. In level 1, the goal was to expand the wall to fill the grid (click lower blue 3 times). In level 2, maybe the goal is to align the figure with the gap.
The figure is at col 18, gap at col 28. I need the figure to move right by 10 columns. At 4 columns per click, that's 2.5 clicks. But clicks are discrete, so maybe 3 clicks would overshoot.
Alternatively, maybe I need to click different blue blocks to get different movement amounts. Let me try clicking a different blue block to see what it does.
Actually, let me first try clicking the lower blue block 2 more times (total 4) and see if the level completes. If not, I'll try other blue blocks.
Wait, let me reconsider. In level 1, the goal was achieved by clicking the lower blue 3 times from the initial state. Here I've clicked the lower blue 2 times and the level isn't done. Maybe I need to click it more times, or maybe I need to click different blue blocks.
Let me try clicking the lower blue 2 more times to see what happens.
